info.name: E-commerce API - Professional Testing Suite 2026
info.description: Complete API testing project covering: REST fundamentals, authentication, error handling, data validation, performance, security, and CI/CD integration. This collection demonstrates all essential skills a QA tester should master in 2026.

exec: // Generate unique email for each test run
pm.environment.set('unique_email', 'testuser_' + Math.random().toString(36).substring(7) + '@example.com');

request.description:
Register a new user account. Demonstrates:
- POST request
- Request body with JSON
- Dynamic email generation
- Response validation
- Saving tokens for authentication

Authenticate user and obtain token. Demonstrates:
- Authentication flows
- Bearer token generation
- Environment variable management

item.name: 2. CRUD OPERATIONS (Products)
item.description: Create, Read, Update, Delete operations on Products resource

request.description:
Retrieve list of all products. Demonstrates:
- GET request
- Bearer token authentication
- Array response validation
- Data type checking
- Business logic validation

item.name: 2.2 GET - Get Single Product by ID

request.description:
Get single product details using dynamic path parameter. Demonstrates:
- Path parameters
- Environment variable usage in URL
- Response validation with variable comparison

exec: // Generate timestamp for unique product name
pm.environment.set('product_timestamp', new Date().getTime());

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Premium Headphones",
  "description": "High-quality wireless headphones with noise cancellation",
  "price": 199.99,
  "category": "Electronics",
  "stock": 50,
  "sku": "HP-{{product_timestamp}}"
}

request.description:
Create new product. Demonstrates:
- POST request with request body
- Request validation (201 status)
- Response validation against sent data
- Saving response data for later use

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Premium Headphones",
  "description": "High-quality wireless headphones with noise cancellation",
  "price": 249.99,
  "category": "Electronics",
  "stock": 75,
  "sku": "HP-{{product_timestamp}}"
}

request.description:
Update existing product. Demonstrates:
- PUT request for full resource update
- Path parameter with variable
- Validating updates were applied
- Ensuring ID immutability

Partially update product (only specific fields). Demonstrates:
- PATCH vs PUT difference
- Partial resource updates
- Field-level validation

Delete a product. Demonstrates:
- DELETE request
- Expected status codes (204, 200)
- Verifying resource removal

Get products with pagination. Demonstrates:
- Query parameters
- Limit and offset pagination
- Parameter validation

Filter products by category. Demonstrates:
- Filter query parameters
- Response data validation

Sort products by price. Demonstrates:
- Sorting parameters
- Validating sorted order

item.name: 4. ERROR HANDLING & NEGATIVE TESTS
item.description: Testing error scenarios and edge cases

request.description:
Test missing authentication token. Demonstrates:
- Negative testing
- Error response validation
- Status code checking (401)

Test requesting non-existent product. Demonstrates:
- 404 error handling
- Error response validation

Test validation errors with invalid data. Demonstrates:
- 400 Bad Request handling
- Input validation
- Error message structure

exec:
pm.test('Status code is 409 Conflict', function() {
  pm.expect([409, 400]).to.include(pm.response.code);

request.description:
Test conflict when creating duplicate. Demonstrates:
- 409 Conflict handling
- Duplicate detection

Add product to shopping cart. Demonstrates:
- POST with nested resources
- Quantity management
- Cart item structure validation

Retrieve current cart. Demonstrates:
- Nested data structure validation
- Mathematical validation (total calculation)
- Array iteration in tests

Update item quantity in cart. Demonstrates:
- PATCH for updates
- Business logic testing

exec:

request.description:
Remove item from cart. Demonstrates:

Create order from cart. Demonstrates:

- Initial state validation
- Saving response for workflow continuation

Process payment for order. Demonstrates:
- Payment flow
- Status code transitions
- Security token handling

Retrieve order status and details. Demonstrates:

- Workflow state tracking

item.name: 7. DATA VALIDATION & EDGE CASES
item.description: Advanced data validation and boundary testing

request.description:
Test maximum price boundary. Demonstrates:
- Boundary value testing
- Edge case validation

exec:

  pm.expect(pm.response.json().stock).to.equal(0);

request.description: Test minimum stock boundary (zero).

{
